subtitle: Main power muscle — aches from long efforts

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Glute max fatigue is a primary cause of form breakdown late in long runs

mild.description: Deep glute soreness after long runs or hill sessions
mild.can_run_note: Yes — keep effort easy for 48 hours after hard sessions
why: Eccentric loading during downhill running causing delayed onset muscle soreness
Glute max underactivation shifting propulsion load to hamstrings and lower back
High volume weeks without adequate strength work to support the load
relief: Foam roll glutes and piriformis — 2 min each side
Figure-4 stretch lying on back 60s each side
Easy walking or cycling to flush out soreness
Cold water immersion or contrast shower post long run
prevention: Glute bridges progressing to single-leg bridges 3× per week
Hip thrusts with load — build to bodyweight × 20 reps
Activation drills before runs: banded clamshells and glute bridges
Reduce downhill pace to maintain glute engagement

moderate.description: Weakness or pain limiting push-off, limping after long runs
moderate.can_run_note: Easy flat runs only — no hills or speed work
why: Glute max strain from sudden effort — sprint finish or steep hill
Proximal hamstring involvement at the ischial tuberosity
Gluteal tendinopathy from sustained compressive loading
relief: Ice or heat depending on whether acute or chronic
Isometric glute squeezes — 10s holds × 10 for tendon pain relief
Reduce sitting on hard surfaces — offloads gluteal tendons
prevention: Progressive heavy glute loading — Romanian deadlifts and hip thrusts
Avoid sitting cross-legged or on hard surfaces during recovery
Eccentric single-leg exercises to build tendon resilience

severe.description: Acute pain with any push-off, cannot complete a run
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — rest and assess
why: Ischial bursitis or avulsion at the tendon insertion
relief: Physiotherapy assessment for grading and rehab plan
Imaging if acute pop was felt during injury
prevention: Progressive loading of gluteal tendon before return to speed work

see_physio_if: A pop or snap was felt at the time of injury
Bruising tracking down the back of the thigh
No improvement after 5 days of rest
